text.title: test-mutation-feedback-loop Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for test-mutation-feedback-loop. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: CI MUST run mutation testing scoped to changed files on every agent-authored PR with break: 70, low: 60, high: 80 thresholds. The job MUST fail (non-zero exit) when the diff mutation score falls below break. Coverage percent alone MUST NOT be used as the merge gate when an LLM authored or modified the tests.
rule.rationale: Coverage measures execution; mutation measures detection. AI-written tests routinely show high coverage with low mutation score because assertions are weak ("not None", "len > 0"). Mutation surfaces every weak assertion as a concrete surviving mutant.

rule.statement: When mutation CI fails, the next agent turn MUST receive (a) the list of surviving mutants with file, line, mutator name, and original-vs-mutated source, and (b) the explicit instruction "add or strengthen assertions that detect each mutant; do not change source under test." The loop MUST terminate either by clearing the threshold or by the agent labelling each remaining mutant as equivalent with a one-line justification committed to mutants.equivalent.md.
rule.rationale: Without the structured surviving-mutants list, the agent re-runs coverage tactics that already failed. With it, the agent has unambiguous targets and converges in 1-3 iterations on real-world legacy retrofit benchmarks (Meta ACH: 73% merge rate at Messenger/WhatsApp).
